FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class BlurHash

Namespace
FluentGpu.Foundation
Assembly
FluentGpu.Foundation.dll

BlurHash decoder — turns a compact ~20–30-char string into a tiny blurred preview (the LQIP placeholder used by Next.js blurDataURL, SwiftUI/Nuke BlurHash). The engine uploads the decoded tile as an image's initial texture so a recognizable blur shows instantly while the full-res art decodes off-thread. Pure math (no deps), AOT-safe. Algorithm: github.com/woltapp/blurhash.

public static class BlurHash

object
BlurHash

Methods

Decode(string?, int, int, Span<byte>, float)

Decode hash into bgraPremul (PREMULTIPLIED BGRA8, opaque, stride = width4; at least widthheight*4 bytes). Returns false on a malformed hash. width× height ≈ 32×32 is plenty (BlurHash is low-frequency; the GPU's linear sampler does the rest).

public static bool Decode(string? hash, int width, int height, Span<byte> bgraPremul, float punch = 1)

Parameters

hash string
width int
height int
bgraPremul Span<byte>
punch float

Returns

bool
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
